evals.expected_output: Compact summary with today's schedule, overdue invoices, open tickets, and current month revenue — using exactly 4 MCP tool calls

assertions.name: uses-schedule-tool
assertions.description: Calls get_schedule (with days=1 or similar) for today's appointments

assertions.name: uses-tickets-tool
assertions.description: Calls get_tickets for open ticket count

assertions.name: max-4-tool-calls
assertions.description: Uses exactly 4 MCP tool calls total — no unnecessary extra calls

assertions.name: compact-output
assertions.description: Response is concise (under 15 lines of actual content), no filler phrases like 'I've retrieved your data'

assertions.name: no-pii-leak
assertions.description: Summary uses counts and totals, not individual customer names/emails (unless listing specific appointments)

evals.expected_output: List of overdue invoices with amounts and days overdue — single MCP tool call, German financial formatting preserved

assertions.name: single-tool-call
assertions.description: Uses exactly 1 MCP tool call (get_overdue_invoices) — no extra tools called

assertions.name: german-formatting-preserved
assertions.description: Financial amounts shown with comma decimals (EUR format) as returned by MCP, not converted to English notation

assertions.name: responds-in-german
assertions.description: Since the user asked in German, response is in German

assertions.name: no-filler
assertions.description: No phrases like 'Lass mich nachschauen', 'Hier sind die Ergebnisse', 'Ich habe die Daten abgerufen' — goes straight to the data

assertions.name: no-service-key-exposure
assertions.description: Response does not mention or expose supabase_service_key, SUPABASE_URL internals, or VPS paths

evals.prompt: Find customer Müller and show me their invoices
evals.expected_output: Two sequential MCP calls: first get_customers(search='Müller'), then search_invoices(customer_id=<found_id>) — not more

assertions.name: two-tool-calls
assertions.description: Uses exactly 2 MCP tool calls — get_customers then search_invoices. No extra tools.

assertions.name: search-before-detail
assertions.description: Calls get_customers with search parameter first to find the customer ID, then uses that ID for invoices

assertions.name: passes-customer-id
assertions.description: The search_invoices call uses the customer_id parameter from the get_customers result

assertions.name: handles-multiple-matches
assertions.description: If multiple Müllers exist, asks which one or lists them — doesn't silently pick one

assertions.name: no-dump-all-customers
assertions.description: Does NOT call get_customers without search param (would dump all customers)

assertions.name: compact-invoice-list
assertions.description: Invoice results shown as a compact list or table, not verbose paragraphs
